body_txt: Want a big, spectacular finale? Want to build a new set? Why not solve both your problems at the same time and trash the set? Even animators are tempted to do this if the background is elaborate enough. May lead to a Rebuilt Set . Not to be confused with Die, Chair, Die! or Tantrum Throwing . Related to Proscenium Reveal and On a Soundstage All Along (please make sure your examples relating to breaking the fourth wall in this way are on those trope pages). Sometimes it's an indication that Nothing Is the Same Anymore . For action shows, it can be caused by a case of All Your Base Are Belong to Us or Storming the Castle . If this is used in terms of driving up the production/story value, then it may be a case of a Finale Production Upgrade . On the other hand, if someone is trying to prevent further production of the show, then you have Torch the Franchise and Run . A justified case of this trope happens in the initial stages of a Home and Garden show, because of the renovations. Compare Anger Montage . As this is an Ending Trope , beware of spoilers .
